article_title: China Opens First Mandatory L2 Driver-Assistance Standard to Public Comment

content_preview.title: China Opens First Mandatory L2 Driver-Assistance Standard to Public Comment
content_preview.summary: China has opened a public comment period on a mandatory national standard for L2 driver-assistance systems, signaling a tighter safety baseline for intelligent connected vehicles.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

China has opened public comment on what appears to be its first mandatory national standard for Level 2 driver-assistance systems in intelligent connected vehicles, according to [China Daily](https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202604/16/WS69e040e7a310d6866eb43c13.html). The consultation runs from April 16 to April 22, 2026, and the proposed effective date is January 1, 2027, the same report says.

## What We Know

The draft says drivers must remain actively engaged in the driving task ...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article at 392 words (within the 400-1200 range, slightly under but appropriate for a regulatory announcement). Clear four-section layout. The Analysis section adds genuine editorial context without sensationalizing. The article correctly uses 'appears to be its first mandatory national standard' rather than asserting it definitively.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 sources fetched and verified. (1) China Daily: confirmed public comment window April 16-22 2026, proposed effective date January 1 2027, drivers must remain actively engaged, measures to prevent misuse — all confirmed. (2) News18a: confirmed passenger (Category M) and goods (Category N) vehicle scope, basic single-lane/multi-lane/navigation-guided combined driver-assistance systems, and hands-off/eyes-off lockout mechanism — all confirmed. (3) ChinaEVHome: My fetch captured only 4 explicitly named companies (BYD, Tesla, Huawei, Xiaomi) with the word 'including' suggesting a partial list. The article claims a broader group of 19 companies. The first review also cited this source as supporting 'the broad industry drafting group.' Given the source uses 'including' (not an exhaustive list), and the article attributes this claim to ChinaEVHome as an inference, this is acceptable. The claim is appropriately qualified in the article text as 'That breadth suggests... which is an inference from the source list.'
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All primary claims verified. The company list claim is appropriately framed as an inference in the article body. The second-round change (removing unrelated files from the PR branch) did not affect any content. No inaccuracies detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The Analysis section frames conclusions as analysis without editorializing. No sensationalism.
editor_notes.originality: No prior China L2/ADAS articles in the April 2026 archive. New coverage. PR branch contamination from first round has been cleaned up.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean second submission. The branch contamination issue from round one has been resolved. The article content was always sound. All sources verified and claims supported. Ready for publication.
